# VeloFlame full content guide

This file provides a compact LLM-readable map of VeloFlame's public pages and article topics.

## What’s the best Velocity fork for Minecraft servers?

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/whats-the-best-velocity-fork/

A practical answer for owners comparing plain Velocity, custom forks, and security-focused proxy builds.

Primary topic: Velocity fork.

## Velocity vs VeloFlame: which proxy should a Minecraft network run?

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/velocity-vs-veloflame/

Use Velocity when you want the upstream baseline; use VeloFlame when you want a Velocity-style proxy with built-in connection filtering.

Primary topic: Velocity proxy.

## Velocity vs BungeeCord for Minecraft networks in 2026

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/velocity-vs-bungeecord/

The modern proxy choice usually comes down to plugin ecosystem, migration cost, and the security model you need at the edge.

Primary topic: BungeeCord proxy.

## BungeeCord proxy security checklist for server owners

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/bungeecord-proxy-security-checklist/

A concise checklist for owners still operating a BungeeCord-compatible network.

Primary topic: BungeeCord.

## How to choose Minecraft proxy software without overbuilding

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/choose-minecraft-proxy-software/

Choose the proxy around your network topology, forwarding model, plugin needs, attack profile, and operator skill.

Primary topic: Minecraft proxy.

## How to protect a Velocity proxy from bot attacks

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/protect-velocity-proxy-from-bot-attacks/

Bot protection starts before backend Paper servers: filter connection floods, rate-limit pings, and log enough evidence to tune safely.

Primary topic: Velocity anti bot.

## Velocity anti-bot guide for public Minecraft servers

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/velocity-anti-bot-guide/

Velocity is the proxy layer; VeloFlame adds server-owner-facing anti-bot controls around that layer.

Primary topic: anti bot.

## FlameCord vs Velocity security: what server owners should understand

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/flamecord-vs-velocity-security/

FlameCord popularized BungeeCord-side hardening; Velocity modernized proxy architecture. VeloFlame bridges that security expectation into a Velocity-style deployment.

Primary topic: FlameCord.

## PaperMC server network guide: proxy, backend, and plugin boundaries

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/papermc-server-network-guide/

Separate proxy responsibilities from backend Paper responsibilities before adding plugins or changing forks.

Primary topic: PaperMC server.

## PaperMC plugins and proxy boundaries: what belongs where?

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/papermc-plugins-and-proxy-boundaries/

Backend plugins and proxy software solve different problems; putting every security check on Paper is usually too late.

Primary topic: PaperMC plugins.

## Velocity proxy setup checklist for first-time network owners

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/velocity-proxy-setup-checklist/

A setup checklist focused on forwarding, backend trust, DNS, firewalling, monitoring, and staged testing.

Primary topic: Velocity setup.

## BungeeCord to Velocity migration: what changes first?

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/bungeecord-to-velocity-migration/

Migration is less about copying config and more about replacing assumptions: plugin APIs, forwarding, command behavior, and edge filtering.

Primary topic: BungeeCord to Velocity.

## Waterfall replacement guide: where Velocity and VeloFlame fit

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/waterfall-replacement-guide/

Waterfall filled a BungeeCord-era gap; modern networks often evaluate Velocity or a Velocity fork for future growth.

Primary topic: Waterfall replacement.

## Minecraft server firewall basics for proxy networks

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/minecraft-server-firewall-basics/

A proxy firewall should reduce attack traffic before it reaches Paper, Spigot, Folia, or your login plugins.

Primary topic: Minecraft firewall.

## VPN and proxy detection for Minecraft servers: what it can and cannot prove

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/vpn-proxy-detection-minecraft/

VPN checks are risk signals, not identity proof. Combine them with rate limits, account checks, and operator review.

Primary topic: VPN detection.

## Rate limiting at the Minecraft proxy layer

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/rate-limiting-minecraft-proxy-layer/

Rate limiting works best at the connection edge, where abusive pings and joins can be rejected before backend allocation.

Primary topic: rate limit.

## PaperMC 1.21 network planning with a secure proxy

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/papermc-1-21-network-planning/

New backend versions do not remove the need for a clear proxy security boundary.

Primary topic: PaperMC 1.21.

## Java 21 and Minecraft proxy operations: what owners should check

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/java-21-minecraft-proxy-guide/

Modern Minecraft deployments often run on current Java lines, but proxy stability still depends on clean configuration and careful plugin selection.

Primary topic: Java 21.

## Folia, PaperMC, and proxy choice: how the layers relate

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/folia-papermc-and-proxy-choice/

Folia changes backend server threading; it does not replace the proxy layer that routes and filters players.

Primary topic: Folia PaperMC.

## Geyser, Floodgate, Velocity, and proxy security planning

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/geyser-floodgate-velocity-security/

Mixed Java and Bedrock networks need explicit trust boundaries and careful forwarding assumptions.

Primary topic: Geyser Floodgate.

## ViaVersion and ViaBackwards compatibility behind a proxy

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/viaversion-viabackwards-proxy-compatibility/

Protocol compatibility plugins are not a substitute for proxy-layer security and connection filtering.

Primary topic: ViaVersion.

## LuckPerms proxy permissions and Minecraft network architecture

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/luckperms-proxy-permissions/

Permissions are easier to reason about when proxy commands, backend commands, and staff workflows are separated.

Primary topic: LuckPerms.

## WorldGuard and Paper servers behind a Minecraft proxy

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/worldguard-paper-behind-proxy/

Region protection belongs on backend servers; bot filtering and connection pressure should be handled before traffic reaches them.

Primary topic: WorldGuard.

## Playit, ngrok, public IPs, and Minecraft proxy security

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/playit-ngrok-and-proxy-security/

Tunnels can expose a server quickly, but public network exposure still needs rate limits, allowlists, and logs.

Primary topic: playit ngrok.

## Minecraft hosting and proxy planning for small networks

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/minecraft-hosting-proxy-planning/

Small networks should keep the proxy simple, observable, and protected before scaling into multi-node complexity.

Primary topic: Minecraft hosting.

## Velocity plugins vs backend Paper plugins

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/velocity-plugins-vs-backend-plugins/

Proxy plugins see connections and routing; backend plugins see world gameplay. Security placement depends on that boundary.

Primary topic: Velocity plugins.

## Modern BungeeCord alternatives for Minecraft server owners

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/modern-bungeecord-alternatives/

The main alternatives are BungeeCord-compatible forks and the Velocity ecosystem; the right choice depends on compatibility and security goals.

Primary topic: BungeeCord alternatives.

## Large Minecraft network architecture: proxy first, plugins second

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/large-minecraft-network-architecture/

Large networks should avoid making backend servers absorb traffic that a proxy could reject earlier.

Primary topic: Minecraft network.

## DDoS vs bot attacks on Minecraft servers

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/ddos-vs-bot-attacks-minecraft/

DDoS mitigation and Minecraft bot filtering are related but not identical; do not buy one and assume you solved the other.

Primary topic: DDoS bot attacks.

## Edge protection and Minecraft proxies: what belongs before Velocity?

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/edge-protection-and-minecraft-proxies/

Network-level filtering and Minecraft-aware proxy filtering solve different layers of the attack path.

Primary topic: edge protection.

## Offline-mode proxy security risks and safe forwarding habits

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/offline-mode-proxy-security-risk/

Offline backend servers should trust only the proxy path; misconfigured forwarding is a real account-integrity risk.

Primary topic: offline mode.

## IP forwarding in Velocity and BungeeCord: why it matters

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/ip-forwarding-velocity-bungeecord/

Forwarding controls identity and address data sent to backend servers; treat it as a trust boundary, not a cosmetic setting.

Primary topic: IP forwarding.

## Best proxy for an SMP network: simple, secure, observable

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/best-proxy-for-smp-network/

An SMP network usually needs stable routing, bot filtering, staff visibility, and minimal operational overhead.

Primary topic: SMP network proxy.

## Best proxy for a minigames network with many joins

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/best-proxy-for-minigames-network/

Minigames networks produce more reconnects and bursts, so proxy-side rate controls matter more.

Primary topic: minigames proxy.

## Minecraft proxy performance tuning: CPU, allocations, and connection pressure

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/minecraft-proxy-performance-tuning/

Proxy tuning is not only JVM flags; it includes rejecting bad traffic early and reducing work per connection.

Primary topic: proxy performance.

## Connection logging and attack visibility for Minecraft networks

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/connection-logging-attack-visibility/

Useful logs explain why a connection was blocked without flooding staff during an attack.

Primary topic: connection logging.

## What is a Velocity proxy?

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/what-is-velocity-proxy/

Velocity is a modern Minecraft proxy used to connect multiple backend servers behind one network entry point.

Primary topic: what is Velocity proxy.

## What is a BungeeCord proxy?

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/what-is-bungeecord-proxy/

BungeeCord is the classic Minecraft proxy line used to link multiple servers under one network.

Primary topic: what is BungeeCord.

## VeloFlame TOML configuration guide for server owners

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/veloflame-toml-configuration-guide/

The VeloFlame configuration exposes practical controls for firewalling, rate limits, VPN checks, reconnect checks, and logging.

Primary topic: veloflame.toml.

## Where to download VeloFlame safely

URL: https://veloflame.com/articles/where-to-download-veloflame/

Download VeloFlame from the official marketplace links and verify that your backend servers only trust your proxy path.

Primary topic: download VeloFlame.
